excerpt: Summing Annex A monthly low/high values for Nov 2020-Jun 2021 yields 8,165-10,486 reported civilian direct deaths.
transform_note: Best estimate is the midpoint of the low/high sum; the source publishes monthly ranges rather than a single E1 total.
confidence_note: Best available E1 civilian direct-death series, but the project states the database underrepresents total casualties.

excerpt: Used as the upper-bound exposed civilian population for E1.
transform_note: Serves as a ceiling scenario before adjusting for out-of-theater displacement.
confidence_note: Rounded population baseline; appropriate for scenario bounds, not a precise daily census.

geographic_scope: Tigray-origin displaced population in Tigray, Afar, Amhara, and eastern Sudan
excerpt: Adding Afar, Amhara, and Sudan figures gives a minimum of 88,530 people outside Tigray by March 2021.
transform_note: Internal IDPs who remained in Tigray are not removed from the civilian denominator; this is an out-of-theater lower bound only.
confidence_note: Lower bound only; Western Tigray expulsions and later movements may raise true out-of-theater absence.

geographic_scope: Tigray Region, Ethiopia (Western Tigray data not provided)
excerpt: Most displacement was internal to Tigray, so internal IDPs remain in the civilian denominator.
transform_note: Used to justify counting internal IDPs as still exposed civilians within the theater.
confidence_note: Published after E1 end date and excludes Western Tigray data, so used as contextual support rather than a direct E1 stock.

excerpt: Opening-war snapshot of government-aligned fighters physically in Tigray.
transform_note: Used as the opening anchor for average in-theater combatant exposure.
confidence_note: Snapshot only; later reinforcement from Eritrean and Amhara forces likely increased average strength.

excerpt: Opening-war estimate for effective Tigray fighters.
transform_note: Combined with government-aligned fighter snapshot to build average combatant exposure scenarios.
confidence_note: Snapshot only; does not capture later mobilization, attrition, or fragmentation.

excerpt: Explicit early-war kill claim; used only as a minimum anchor.
transform_note: Not treated as an episode total; it is only an opening-war lower-bound anchor.

excerpt: 1,000 released plus more than 5,000 still held implies at least 6,000 captured government soldiers.
transform_note: Not converted into deaths; used only as a scale-of-losses signal for military modeling.
confidence_note: Belligerent claim; Reuters could not independently confirm.

inflicting_side: Samri youth group with local security forces
excerpt: EHRC-attributed massacre of civilians in Mai Kadra.
transform_note: Single event; not an episode total.
confidence_note: Strong side-attributed atrocity claim, but still one event within a larger episode.

excerpt: HRW lower-bound event estimate for the Axum massacre.
transform_note: Lower-bound phrasing retained; no upward adjustment made.
confidence_note: Strong side attribution, but still a lower-bound event-specific estimate.

inflicting_side: Amhara forces / Ethiopian allies (claim as reported)
excerpt: Excluded from death counts because the article used 'destroyed' rather than killed and Reuters could not verify the claim.
confidence_note: Illustrates why ambiguous casualty language should stay outside the core direct-death path.
